features.title: Tool Tier Registry JSON
features.description: Create .specify/doctor-registry.json defining required, recommended, and optional tools with binary names, version flags, aliases, project types, and platform-specific install commands. Source of truth in scaffold at .claude-plugin/scaffold/common/.specify/doctor-registry.json.
testing_steps: Verify .claude-plugin/scaffold/common/.specify/doctor-registry.json exists and is valid JSON (jq empty < file)
Verify each entry has required fields: name, purpose, tier, project_types, version_flag, aliases, install
Verify install object has darwin, linux, and generic string fields for every entry
Verify at least 3 entries with tier=required exist: jq, git, python3
Verify recommended entries cover Node (npm, npx, prettier), Python (ruff), Rust (cargo, rustfmt), Go (gofmt, go), Shell (shellcheck, shfmt)
Verify optional entries include markdownlint-cli2, golangci-lint, black, autopep8, rubocop, google-java-format
Verify tier field is one of: required, recommended, optional for every entry
Verify project_types is an empty array for all required-tier entries
Verify python3 entry has aliases array containing python
Verify all install.darwin commands reference brew where applicable

features.title: Project Type Auto-Detection
features.description: Implement detect_project_types() function in doctor.sh that scans the project root and one level of subdirectories for config files (package.json, Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, go.mod, Gemfile, build.gradle, pom.xml) and user-authored .sh files to determine which project types are present.
testing_steps: Run doctor.sh in the CPF repo and verify it detects node project type (package.json exists)
Create a temporary directory with a Cargo.toml and verify rust is detected
Create a temporary directory with a pyproject.toml and verify python is detected
Create a temporary directory with a go.mod and verify go is detected
Create a temporary directory with a Gemfile and verify ruby is detected
Create a temporary directory with a build.gradle and verify java is detected
Verify a directory with only scripts/install-hooks.sh does NOT detect shell type
Create a directory with a custom .sh file at the root and verify shell IS detected
Create a directory with both package.json and go.mod and verify both node and go are detected
Verify detection searches one level of subdirectories (e.g., subdir/package.json triggers node)

features.title: Tool Presence and Version Check
features.description: Implement check_tool() function that runs command -v for each tool in the registry, checks aliases if primary binary not found, runs the version_flag command to capture version info, and returns PASS/FAIL/WARN/INFO status based on tier and project type relevance.
testing_steps: Run doctor.sh and verify git (universally installed) shows PASS with a version string
Verify a tool with version_flag=null shows PASS without a version string when found
Verify a missing required tool shows FAIL status
Verify a missing recommended tool (for a detected project type) shows WARN status
Verify a missing optional tool shows INFO status
Verify a tool for a non-detected project type is skipped entirely (not in output)
Verify python3 alias fallback: if python3 is not found, check python as alias
Verify version_flag failure shows found with version unknown rather than crashing
Verify the jq bootstrap check runs before registry parsing with hardcoded install hints
Verify the jq bootstrap check exits with code 1 and prints darwin and linux install commands

features.title: Compliance Report Output
features.description: Implement compliance report in two output formats: text (human-readable, default) and JSON (machine-parseable). Both contain identical data: platform, project types, per-tool results with status/version/install hints, summary counts, and overall readiness. Text groups by tier with PASS/WARN/FAIL/INFO labels. JSON outputs a single object with tools array, summary object, and ready boolean.
testing_steps: Run doctor.sh --output=text and verify output contains header, platform line, and Required heading
Verify text output groups tools under correct tier headings with 2-space indent
Verify text output uses fixed-width status labels: PASS, WARN, FAIL, INFO, SKIP
Verify missing tools show platform-specific install commands on 8-space indented continuation line
Verify text summary line shows correct pass/warn/fail counts
Verify text shows READY when all required tools pass, NOT READY when any fail
Verify empty tiers are omitted from text output
Run doctor.sh --output=json and verify output is valid JSON (pipe to jq empty)
Verify JSON output has top-level fields: platform, project_types, tools, summary, ready
Verify JSON tools array entries have fields: name, tier, status, version, install_hint
Verify JSON status values are one of: pass, warn, fail, info, skip
Verify JSON version is null for missing tools and null for tools with no version_flag
Verify JSON install_hint is null for present tools and a string for missing tools
Verify JSON summary has passed, warnings, failures integer fields that sum to total tools
Verify JSON ready is true when failures==0, false otherwise
Verify text and JSON contain the same tools with the same statuses
Verify exit code is 0 when all required tools pass (both formats)
Verify exit code is 1 when any required tool is missing (both formats)
Verify --output=text is the default when no --output flag is given

features.title: Standalone doctor.sh Script
features.description: Create scripts/doctor.sh as a self-contained bash script with set -euo pipefail, argument parsing for --output=text|json, --registry, and --project-dir flags, project root detection via git rev-parse, and the complete doctor pipeline: bootstrap jq check, detect platform, detect project types, iterate registry, check tools, output report in requested format.
testing_steps: Verify .claude-plugin/scaffold/common/scripts/doctor.sh exists and is executable
Run scripts/doctor.sh with no arguments from the CPF repo root and verify it completes with text output
Run scripts/doctor.sh --output=json and verify valid JSON output
Run scripts/doctor.sh --registry /nonexistent and verify it errors about missing registry
Run scripts/doctor.sh --project-dir /tmp and verify it uses /tmp as the project root
Verify --registry with a relative path resolves against the effective project root, not CWD
Verify the script uses git rev-parse --show-toplevel for default project root with PWD fallback
Verify the script starts with set -euo pipefail and includes a bash shebang
Run shellcheck on the script and verify zero errors
Verify the script completes in under 2 seconds on a standard workstation
Verify the jq bootstrap error is plain text to stderr even when --output=json is requested
Verify the script handles being run from a subdirectory of the project (finds project root correctly)

features.title: Init Integration with Doctor
features.description: Update the /cpf:specforge init sub-command in SKILL.md to run scripts/doctor.sh after install-hooks.sh and version tracking. Display the compliance report visually separated from the init summary. Include a recheck reminder line.
testing_steps: Read SKILL.md init workflow and verify doctor is listed as a step after install-hooks.sh
Verify init instructions include running scripts/doctor.sh
Verify init instructions include the recheck reminder text mentioning both /cpf:specforge doctor and scripts/doctor.sh
Verify init instructions state doctor output should be visually separated from file counts
Verify init instructions state that doctor failures do not block init completion

features.title: Specforge Doctor Skill Sub-Command
features.description: Add /cpf:specforge doctor sub-command to SKILL.md that runs scripts/doctor.sh --output=text via the Bash tool for human-readable display, and optionally scripts/doctor.sh --output=json for structured parsing. Displays stdout output to the user and summarizes missing required tools if the script exits with code 1. Add doctor to argument-hint frontmatter.
testing_steps: Verify SKILL.md contains a ### /cpf:specforge doctor section
Verify the doctor section includes Purpose, Workflow, and Notes subsections
Verify the workflow instructs running scripts/doctor.sh from the project root
Verify the workflow instructs displaying the script output to the user
Verify the workflow instructs summarizing missing required tools on exit code 1
Verify the SKILL.md argument-hint frontmatter includes doctor

features.title: Register Doctor Files in Upgrade Tiers
features.description: Add doctor-registry.json and scripts/doctor.sh to the overwrite tier in upgrade-tiers.json so they are always replaced on /cpf:specforge upgrade.
testing_steps: Read upgrade-tiers.json and verify .specify/doctor-registry.json is in the overwrite array
Read upgrade-tiers.json and verify scripts/doctor.sh is in the overwrite array
Verify both entries are strings matching the exact projected paths in host projects

features.title: Doctor Self-Test on CPF Repo
features.description: Verify doctor works correctly on the CPF source repo itself. The repo is a Node + Shell project (package.json exists, user-authored .sh files exist). All required tools should be present on the development machine.
testing_steps: Run scripts/doctor.sh from the CPF repo root and capture output
Verify output detects node as a project type
Verify output lists jq, git, python3 under Required heading
Verify output lists npm, npx, prettier under Recommended heading (node project)
Verify output shows shellcheck and shfmt as recommended if shell type is detected
Verify the summary line shows zero failures on a fully equipped dev machine
Verify the overall assessment is READY
Verify scripts/doctor.sh exits with code 0
Verify the output includes version strings for tools that have version_flag set
Run /cpf:specforge doctor (skill invocation) and verify it displays the same report

features.title: Update README and CHANGELOG
features.description: Add doctor to the README.md workflow table and usage sections. Add a feature entry to CHANGELOG.md for the new sub-command.
testing_steps: Verify README.md workflow table includes a doctor row
Verify README.md mentions scripts/doctor.sh as a standalone option
Verify CHANGELOG.md has an entry for the doctor feature under the Unreleased or next version section
Run markdownlint on README.md and CHANGELOG.md and verify zero errors
